evals.name: pr-code-review
evals.prompt:
Review the following diff for a new Zsh function. Provide a technical review following the required structure.

diff --git a/src/zsh/functions/pkg.zsh b/src/zsh/functions/pkg.zsh
index 1234567..89abcdef 100644
--- a/src/zsh/functions/pkg.zsh
+++ b/src/zsh/functions/pkg.zsh
@@ -10,6 +10,12 @@
 function pkg_install() {
   local pkg=$1
   if [[ -z $pkg ]]; then
-    echo "Usage: pkg_install <package>"
+    echo "Error: No package specified"
     return 1
   fi
-  sudo apt-get install $pkg
+  # Install the package
+  sudo apt-get install -y $pkg
+  if [[ $? -ne 0 ]]; then
+    echo "Failed to install $pkg"
+  fi
 }
evals.expected_output: A technical review with structured findings (Severity, Description, Impact, Proposed Solution) and a neutral tone.

evals.name: architecture-design-review
evals.prompt:
Review this proposed architecture for a new logging service. Focus on scalability and reliability.

Architecture Proposal:
- Use a single central Node.js server to receive all logs via HTTP POST.
- Store logs in a local JSON file on the server.
- If the JSON file exceeds 1GB, rotate it by renaming it to logs.old.json and creating a new logs.json.
- Provide a simple GET endpoint to download the current logs.json file.
evals.expected_output: A technical review identifying flaws like single point of failure and scalability limits, using the required structured format.
